assessments.status_rationale: Today’s items focus on the Iran war, DHS funding, and a Supreme Court birthright-citizenship case; there is no referenced news about an executive order limiting mail-in voting or any federal court TRO/preliminary injunction against it.
No news in today’s bullets indicates additional states joining lawsuits challenging a federal executive order limiting mail-in voting, nor provides an updated count toward the 15-state threshold.
There is no mention in today’s bullets of CISA issuing an alert/advisory referencing a malicious NPM 'axios' package compromise or publishing related IOCs/mitigations.

No announcement is cited today from NPM or GitHub about implementing new default supply-chain security controls (mandatory provenance attestation/signing or mandatory 2FA for maintainers of very large packages).

A MarketWatch item reports Oracle cutting ~30,000 workers, which is consistent with restructuring activity, but it does not provide evidence of a disclosed GAAP restructuring-related charge of at least $1.0B in a single quarter tied to layoffs/reorganization in a 10-Q/10-K or earnings release.
Today’s bullets do not mention Microsoft updating full-year capex guidance or attributing any increase to AI/data-center capacity needs.
No reference appears today to the 2026 Social Security Trustees Report or to any projected OASI trust fund depletion year.
No news in today’s bullets concerns the UN Security Council’s UNIFIL renewal resolution or any strengthened language on IED threats, force protection, or freedom of movement.
There is no mention today of ASTM/ISO publishing microplastics sampling/analysis guidance addressing glove contamination and mitigation recommendations.
